Downstream's office at 3571 Far West Blvd, Suite 103 sits in zip 78731 — an exact match to the donor's zip — and the filing's employer ('Downstream Investments') and occupation ('Investor') align precisely. He is one of four partners at Crestmoor Capital Partners (Denver-based, with Dallas and Austin offices), which repositions problem loans and assets in the real estate, energy, and operating-business sectors, and serves as Chairman of Titleist Asset Management; his portfolio focus is Titleist, Crestmoor and Agricapture. The corroborating chain: his agriculture interests match the filing's occupation ('Rancher') and employer ('Baker Cattle Company'); his philanthropy includes Cattlemen Care — the cattle-industry charity founded in 2007 by his late wife Allison — along with the Boys and Girls Club of Austin and the Colorado River Foundation; and Allison was killed in an auto-pedestrian accident in Tarrytown, the central-Austin neighborhood that makes up zip 78703, the donor's zip. Confidence is medium rather than high because no source directly names him as owner of an entity called 'Baker Cattle Company' (no Texas SOS or company record surfaced), 'Steve Baker' is a common name, and the Tarrytown link runs through the accident location rather than a confirmed residential address. Industry is recorded as Self-Employed because the taxonomy has no Agriculture label and he is an owner-operator of his own cattle company rather than a practicing attorney per his own disclosure. His unspecified 'energy' interests are noted but no oil & gas executive, board or ownership role is documented, so no oil_gas flag is recorded. Gave $900 to Kirk Watson in fall 2022; no FEC donation history.
